audit.note: Pre-archive audit-drift ran inline as part of /opsx:archive Step 3.5. Cat 2 (lens staleness) degenerate (no openspec/lenses/). Cat 4 (archive coherence) vacuously satisfied (no prior archives in this project). Cat 1 (vocab residue) surfaced 3 '~830/~200 lines' references — all classified as historical-fidelity preserved (explore.md and proposal.md as the design record at proposal time; archived review JSON as audit trail). Cat 3 (cross-doc consistency) found 2 real drifts in README: test count claim (93 → 96) and commit-arc enumeration (10 items → 15+). Both fixed inline pre-archive. Per orbit's 'audit findings are informational, not gate' convention, archive would have proceeded either way; inline fix was preference for clean close-out.

sync_specs.note: Greenfield case: openspec/specs/ was empty at archive time. All 4 capability deltas were pure ADDED Requirements; each became a freshly-created canonical baseline at openspec/specs/<capability>/spec.md. 19 requirements total now baseline (4 + 8 + 3 + 4 across the four capabilities). Future changes will delta against these.

warnings: Proposal warning (non-blocking, from openspec validate): 'Consider splitting changes with more than 10 deltas'. For bootstrap-orbit-status-cli the deltas total 19 across 4 capabilities; intentionally bundled into one change because it's the foundational bootstrap of orbit-status itself, and splitting greenfield capability creation across multiple changes adds ceremony without benefit. The pattern matches the bootstrap-openspec-orbit precedent (also a single multi-capability bootstrap change). Future feature additions to orbit-status are expected to split per capability.

github_issues_filed_during_change.title: v0.1 test coverage gap: ~15 spec scenarios untested (acknowledged trade-off, tracking for future closure)
Overlay ships unmodified upstream files — should be delta-only
Add convention/flag: AI should not auto-invoke opsx commands without explicit authorization
Workflow commands (explore/propose/apply/verify) should emit run-summary JSONs with next_recommended
propose command should recommend /opsx:review next, not /opsx:apply (orbit workflow has a proposal-mode review step)
/opsx:address-reviews should auto-discover the most recent internal review JSON for the same scope
/opsx:address-reviews should default to per-finding walk with full detail (regardless of input source); add explicit batch mode for bulk processing
Review commands should dynamically recommend the next reviewer mode based on iteration history (and offer clean-vs-seeded external)
Document the review-mode decision framework (in-context vs --fresh vs external)
Make /opsx:address-reviews auto-cascade ripple-flagged files by default (reverse the v1 "list, don't edit" decision)
Workflow inflection points should list available next-step options + recommended action (generalizes #12)

external_reviewers.findings: 0C / 4W / 5S — all resolved via iter-1 address-reviews + iter-3 ripple cleanup

external_reviewers.model: GPT-5 Codex (fresh session, different from same-AI-internal)
external_reviewers.findings: 0C / 3W / 0S — all resolved + 1 side-effect bug discovered + 3 regression tests added

context.meta_validation: The bootstrap change implementing orbit-status was reviewed using orbit's own review commands (orbit-orbit-orbit dogfooding — orbit-status being built using openspec-orbit which itself was bootstrapped using openspec). The anchoring-vs-fresh-context discipline was validated by example: 3 of 3 external system-review findings were missed by the in-context system review. The dogfooding surfaced 11 issues filed against orbit and orbit-status combined, providing concrete real-world fuel for upstream improvement work.
